formula_note: Model-generated military/combatant deaths inside the DK direct-violence episode. Floors come from Tuol Po Chrey and S-21; upper range allows for wider former-Khmer-Republic and intra-Khmer-Rouge soldier/cadre killings noted qualitatively in DC-Cam.
uncertainty_note: Very uncertain because published violent-death totals are mixed-status and because many purged party victims were not clearly combatants.
quality_note: No clean national combatant death ledger was found.

formula_note: Residual civilian deaths from a total direct-violent-death envelope of 500k / 850k / 1.1m after subtracting model-generated military deaths and an explicit unknown-status bucket.
uncertainty_note: Civilian deaths dominate in absolute terms, but the exact civilian share is unstable because many prison and mass-grave victims are status-ambiguous.
quality_note: Civilian count is not directly published as a national direct-death series for this episode.

formula_note: Explicit unknown-status retention for status-mixed detainees, grave victims, and ambiguous cadre/combatant cases. This bucket is intentionally large to avoid false precision.
uncertainty_note: If future archival work better separates officials, armed cadres, and civilians, this bucket should shrink and the DR should be recalculated.
quality_note: Unknown-status handling is a major driver of uncertainty.

formula_note: Approximate exposure over 44.75 months using scenario-average regular-force strengths of roughly 72k / 84k / 96k, anchored to April 1975 (68k), January 1976 (72,248), and October 1978 (>100k).
uncertainty_note: This excludes a precise reconstruction of remnant FANK exposure and does not fully add foreign forces, so it is best seen as a Khmer-Rouge-centered military exposure denominator.
quality_note: Reasonably anchored, but still an interpolation rather than a full roster series.

formula_note: Linear-exposure approximation over 44.75 months. Starts at 7.3m / 7.894m / 8.102m. Ends are scenario-adjusted from the January 1980 population of 6.4m to about 6.4m / 6.7m / 6.9m for January 1979, reflecting uncertainty over the 1979 famine toll.
uncertainty_note: Because monthly population counts do not exist, this is a scenario-based exposure approximation rather than a direct person-month series.
quality_note: National episode allows a defensible denominator, but the end-point must be back-cast from 1980.

formula_note: DR_v1_direct = (mil_deaths_direct / mil_person_months) / (civ_deaths_direct / civ_person_months). Low = (mil_low / mil_pm_high) / (civ_high / civ_pm_low) = (20,000 / 4,295,754) / (820,000 / 306,519,918) = 1.74. Best = (mil_best / mil_pm_best) / (civ_best / civ_pm_best) = (50,000 / 3,758,784) / (690,000 / 326,522,021) = 6.29. High = (mil_high / mil_pm_low) / (civ_low / civ_pm_high) = (100,000 / 3,221,815) / (420,000 / 335,650,497) = 24.80.
uncertainty_note: Interpret as a high-uncertainty ratio. A value above 1 here does not mean civilians were a minority of victims; it reflects a much smaller exposed military population plus concentrated purges of armed personnel and former soldiers.
quality_note: Core status split is model-generated from mixed-status evidence.
